• payable amounts are calculated correctly • invalid or duplicate voucher creation is prevented 11.3.7 Invoice Management The module is acceptable when: • invoices can be generated from valid jobs • invoice amounts match underlying service data • invalid invoice operations are rejected • void operations preserve history 11.3.8 Receipt Management The module is acceptable when: • receipts can be recorded against valid invoices • overpayment is prevented • invoice balances are updated correctly 11.3.9 Reporting The module is acceptable when: • reports reflect accurate operational and financial data • filters and selection criteria behave correctly • no misleading or incomplete data is returned as valid results 11.4 Business Flow Acceptance Criteria End-to-End Flow: Job to Payment The system is acceptable when: 1. customer is created or selected 2. job card is created
--- PAGE 109 ---

• The system must allow voiding of invoices under valid conditions Expected Outcome: Customer billing is handled in a structured and controlled manner. BR-11: Proforma Invoice Management The system must support preliminary billing through proforma invoices. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of proforma invoices • The system must allow conversion of proforma invoices into final invoices Expected Outcome: Preliminary billing can be performed prior to final financial processing. BR-12: Receipt Management The system must support recording of payments received from customers. Requirements: • The system must allow creation of receipts • The system must allow linking of receipts to invoices • The system must support voiding of receipts Expected Outcome: Payments are accurately recorded and linked to corresponding financial documents. BR-13: Reporting and Analytics The system must provide reporting capabilities for operational and financial data. Requirements: • The system must generate operational reports • The system must generate historical reports

5. Voucher is created Main Flow 1. User selects batch 2. System calculates payable amount 3. User creates payment voucher 4. System validates batch 5. Voucher is created Postconditions • Payment obligation recorded 5.7 Process 6: Invoice Generation Process Name Customer Billing Trigger User initiates billing for a job Preconditions • Job is valid • Cost is finalized Main Flow 1. User selects job 2. System retrieves job cost 3. User creates invoice 4. System validates job

5. Invoice is generated Exception Flows E1: Invalid Job • Invoice creation fails Postconditions • Invoice created and ready for payment 5.8 Process 7: Receipt Processing Process Name Payment Recording Trigger Customer makes payment Preconditions • Invoice exists Main Flow 1. User selects invoice 2. User records payment 3. System validates amount 4. Receipt is created Exception Flows E1: Overpayment • System rejects receipt Postconditions
--- PAGE 59 ---

Explanation: Subsequent correction should occur through controlled actions such as voiding or issuance of replacement records if supported. BRULE-47: Voided Invoice Preservation Voiding an invoice must preserve the invoice record and its historical relationship rather than erase it. BRULE-48: Proforma as Non-Final Billing A proforma invoice must be treated as a preliminary billing document and not as a finalized financial settlement record. BRULE-49: Proforma Conversion Control Where conversion from proforma invoice to final invoice is supported, the conversion must preserve business consistency and avoid duplicated billing. BRULE-50: Duplicate Billing Prevention The system must prevent double invoicing of the same billable content unless explicitly allowed by the business model. 4.12 Receipt Rules BRULE-51: Valid Invoice Requirement for Receipt A receipt must be linked to a valid payable invoice or supported financial source. BRULE-52: Receipt Amount Limit The cumulative total of receipts applied to an invoice must not exceed the allowed receivable amount for that invoice unless overpayment handling is explicitly supported. BRULE-53: Voided or Invalid Target Restriction The system must prevent receipt application to a voided, invalid, or otherwise non-receivable financial document unless business rules explicitly permit it.

• duplicate invoice generation • duplicate voucher creation • duplicate receipt registration for the same transaction intent 9.5.7 Dependency Failure Exceptions Where a required dependency such as the database or supporting service is unavailable, the system must fail safely. Required Behavior • avoid partial commit where possible • return controlled failure response • log the failure for support investigation • allow recovery procedures where applicable 9.5.8 Unexpected Internal Exceptions Where an unforeseen system error occurs, the API must prevent sensitive internal details from being exposed while still ensuring that the failure is recorded for diagnosis. Required Behavior • return generic internal error response • log diagnostic details securely • preserve transaction integrity 9.6 Exception Handling Principles by Business Area 9.6.1 Master Data Exceptions Failures in customer, vendor, or event processing must not break downstream integrity. Invalid creates or updates must be rejected before dependent records are affected.
